channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Swalwell slammed on social media for questioning how Trump will lower grocery prices
item.description: Rep. Eric Swalwell, D-Calif., took aim at President-elect Trump in a post on X in which he questioned how the cost of groceries would go down.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Texas Medical Board director retires after uproar over his Planned Parenthood employment
item.description: Conservative lawmakers claimed credit for Dr. Robert Bredt’s retirement after it came out he also worked at a Planned Parenthood-affiliated lab.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Eleanor Klibanoff, KPRC 2

item.title: House Republican bill would pave way for Trump to acquire Panama Canal for US
item.description: Rep. Dusty Johnson (R-S.D.) will introduce a bill that would authorize the president to purchase the Panama Canal and put it under U.S. control, an acquisition that President-elect Trump has been pushing over the last several weeks.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Matt Gaetz mulls Florida gubernatorial bid
item.description: Former Rep. Matt Gaetz, R-Fla., has indicated that he is mulling the possibility of mounting a Florida gubernatorial bid.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: These Democrats flipped their votes on the Laken Riley Act
item.description: Seven House Democrats flipped their votes to "yes" on a GOP-led bill named after the slain Georgia student Laken Riley that would require detention of migrants arrested for theft.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Durbin asks DOJ to clarify scope of president’s military power ahead of Trump inauguration
item.description: Sen. Dick Durbin (D-Ill.), the top Democrat on the Senate Judiciary Committee, is urging the Department of Justice (DOJ) to rescind a number of internal opinions dealing with a president’s military powers while asking for clarification on domestic use of America’s forces.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Germany's leader says all countries must respect existing borders, in a rebuttal of Trump
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — German Chancellor Olaf Scholz said Wednesday that the principle of inviolability of borders applies to every country, however powerful, and suggested that expansionist comments by U.S. President-elect Donald Trump are meeting with “incomprehension” among European leaders.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Justice Department says it plans to release only part of special counsel's Trump report for now
item.description: The Justice Department says it will release special counsel Jack Smith’s findings on Donald Trump’s efforts to undo the results of the 2020 presidential election but will keep the rest of the report under wraps for now.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker And Alanna Durkin Richer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: IRS has improved taxpayer services but is slow to resolve ID theft, an independent watchdog says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The IRS boosted taxpayer services through Democrats’ Inflation Reduction Act but still faces processing claims from a coronavirus pandemic-era tax credit program and is slow to resolve certain identity theft cases, according to an independent watchdog report released Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump files emergency petition to Supreme Court to prevent sentencing in NY v. Trump
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump on Wednesday morning filed an emergency petition to the United States Supreme Court in an effort to block his sentencing in New York v. Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump asks the Supreme Court to block sentencing in his hush money case in New York
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is asking the Supreme Court to call off Friday’s sentencing in his hush money case in New York.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Ex-FBI informant who fabricated bribery story about Biden and his son Hunter gets 6 years in prison
item.description: A former FBI informant who fabricated a story about President Joe Biden and his son Hunter accepting bribes has been sentenced to six years in prison.
item.dc:creator: Alanna Durkin Richer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Colleges prepare for new legal and political terrain under Trump
item.description: Colleges and universities are preparing for new legal and political territory as President-elect Trump returns to office. Concerns for international students, GOP attacks on higher education and increased protests on campus are just some of the worries on administrators' minds.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Colorado governor walks a tightrope in approaching Trump. Deportations likely to be the first test
item.description: DENVER (AP) — Colorado Gov. Jared Polis donned safety glasses and seized the handle of the electric saw, guiding the buzzing blade down through a stack of printed-out executive orders dating back decades.
item.dc:creator: JESSE BEDAYN and NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Disgraced former US Rep. George Santos seeks to delay fraud sentencing to make more podcast episodes
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Disgraced former congressman George Santos has asked a New York judge to delay his sentencing on federal fraud charges until the summer so he can pay off more than half a million dollars in fines by making more episodes of his recently launched podcast “Pants on Fire.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 2 death row inmates reject Biden's commutation of sentences
item.description: Shannon Agofsky and Len Davis believe the life sentences put them at a legal disadvantage with them planning to seek appeals based on claims of innocence.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, NewsNation

item.title: Takeaways from Trump’s Mar-a-Lago press conference
item.description: In a press conference at his Mar-a-Lago club in Florida, Trump would not rule out using military force to seize control of the Panama Canal and Greenland, citing national security interests.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: What to know about Trump and his keen interest in Greenland
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has said multiple times that the U.S. should buy Greenland, an autonomous territory of Denmark. The sparsely populated island is geopolitically important and mineral-rich.
item.dc:creator: Chandelis Duster, MPR News

item.title: Trump refuses to rule out use of military force to take control of Greenland, Panama Canal
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump on Tuesday said he would not rule out the use of military force to seize control of the Panama Canal and Greenland, as he declared U.S. control of both to be vital to American national security.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and ZEKE MILLER Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: U.K. leader Starmer slams ‘lies and misinformation’ after attacks from Elon Musk
item.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer on Monday condemned "lies and misinformation" that he said are undermining U.K. democracy, in response to a barrage of attacks on his government from Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Rudy Giuliani is held in contempt of court in $148 million defamation case
item.description: Rudy Giuliani was found in contempt of court for failing to properly respond to requests for information as he turned over assets to satisfy a defamation ruling granted to two Georgia poll workers.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump Jr. plans Greenland visit after father stirs controversy
item.description: Donald Trump Jr. is planning to head to Greenland for a day-long trip just weeks after his father, President-elect Trump, said U.S. ownership of the Danish territory "is an absolute necessity.
item.dc:creator: Sarakshi Rai, Fox 5

item.title: Trump to meet with Senate Republicans on Wednesday
item.description: President-elect Trump will be on Capitol Hill on Wednesday at the invitation of Senate Republican leadership, according to three sources familiar with the invitation.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Judge holds Giuliani in contempt in Georgia election workers case
item.description: A federal judge held Rudy Giuliani in civil contempt on Monday for failing to comply with court orders in two former Georgia election workers’ efforts to collect their $146 million defamation judgment. At the end of a two-day hearing that saw Giuliani take the stand, U.S.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Pence: 'Admirable' that Harris certified Trump's win after losing election
item.description: Former Vice President Mike Pence, who had to be evacuated four years ago Monday as rioters stormed the Capitol, welcomed "the return of order and civility" as Congress moved to certify President-elect Donald Trump's 2024 victory.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Trump lawyers push to halt Jan. 10 hush money case sentencing
item.description: President-elect Trump’s legal team urged a judge to hold off on sentencing him on Jan. 10 on his New York criminal conviction ahead of his inauguration.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: Trump is certified as the 2024 election winner without challenge, in stark contrast to 2021 violence
item.description: Lawmakers convened under heavy security and a snowstorm to certify the vote, with the reading of the electoral results from the states coming swiftly.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Congress formally certifies Trump's election win
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) plowed ahead with the vote despite a snowstorm set to hit Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Nippon, U.S. Steel file suit after Biden administration blocks $15 billion deal
item.description: Nippon Steel and U.S. Steel are filing a federal lawsuit challenging the Biden administration’s decision to block a proposed nearly $15 billion deal for Nippon to acquire Pittsburgh-based U.S. Steel.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: A Pulitzer winner quits Washington Post after a cartoon on Bezos is killed
item.description: Washington Post cartoonist Ann Telnaes resigned after an editor rejected her sketch satirizing tech chiefs, including the Post's owner and Amazon founder Jeff Bezos.
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik, MPR News

item.title: Trump will be sentenced Jan. 10 in New York case, days before his inauguration
item.description: In a decision Friday, New York Judge Juan Merchan noted that his inclination was to not impose a sentence of incarceration.
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo, MPR News

item.title: Nathan Fletcher's accuser dismisses sexual assault allegations from lawsuit
item.description: The former Metropolitan Transit System employee who accused Nathan Fletcher of sexual misconduct in a bombshell lawsuit that propelled his resignation from office has walked back some of her most serious allegations in new court filings.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5
